mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending momentum; rising RSAFS drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the absence of near-term tightening risk pushes equity risk premiums lower, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals coordinated expansion across labor and supply, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint z-score of PAYEMS and INDPRO changes fails to produce Rank IC > 0.03 versus 5-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0% and has fallen over the prior three months, continued labor market healing without immediate wage-pressure tightening lifts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.0% and UNRATE lower than 3 months prior
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of instances when UNRATE is above 5% and declining over the prior three months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing profitability and softening corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals accelerating construction activity and downstream demand for materials and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST fail to associate with positive 5-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.0% signals diminishing labor slack and supports household income expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 6.0% is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year window.
